route.name: Anza-Hapaha Loop
route.region: Anza-Borrego Desert State Park / Borrego Springs
route.description: Official/public GPX desert loop through Anza-Borrego terrain with sand, dirt roads, washes, pavement connectors, and serious water exposure.
route.surfaceMix: Official/public GPX desert loop through Anza-Borrego terrain with sand, dirt roads, washes, pavement connectors, and serious water exposure.
route.bestSeason: Cool season only; July heat is a blocker for normal trip planning.

difficultyProfile.surface: Official/public GPX desert loop through Anza-Borrego terrain with sand, dirt roads, washes, pavement connectors, and serious water exposure.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Water is sparse and must be carried or confirmed source-by-source; July capacity requirements are severe.
resources.food: Borrego Springs and start/end services; carry all route food unless a detour is planned.
resources.camping: Anza-Borrego camping and backcountry rules must be checked for the exact itinerary.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before gate confirmation.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Keep as a cool-season California desert route.
Do not promote for next weekend.
Build a water and emergency plan before any future attempt.

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control gate confirmation.

days.name: Anza-Hapaha Loop segment 1
days.camp: Anza-Borrego camping and backcountry rules must be checked for the exact itinerary.

waypoints.name: Stagecoach RV resort; General store hours posted 9-5 Tuesday-Saturday (https://www.stagecoachtrails.com/general-store/)
This 4-mile downhill stretch of road can be busy at times (trucks and toy-haulers), and there is no shoulder. Wear bright clothing and use lights.
Barrel Spring: water source at PCT crossing; park your bike and walk to spring
Use lights and bright colors; just a mile on this road, but there's no shoulder
Anza-Borrego Desert State Park Visitor's Center

logistics: Final gate confirmation requires live date-specific resource and legal checks.
